records.title: From Within
records.summary: Youth Artist Residents exhibition reflecting on personal experiences, memories, and environments that shape each artist's creative voice.
records.description: Presented in the vitrine gallery, From Within highlights the work of Oolite Arts' Youth Artist Residents. Developed over the course of the Youth Artist Residency, the exhibition marks a period of experimentation, reflection, and growth through drawing, painting, sculpture, and mixed media. Featuring works by Ana Blanco, Noa Garcia, Emely Yanji, Melina Walsh, and TJ Wright.
records.featuredArtists: Ana Blanco; Noa Garcia; Emely Yanji; Melina Walsh; TJ Wright

records.title: Sites of the Self
records.summary: Studio Residents exhibition examining how identity is shaped, negotiated, and continually redefined.
records.description: Oolite Arts is pleased to present Sites of the Self, curated by René Morales, bringing together works by the current cohort of Oolite Studio Residents to examine the processes through which identity is shaped, negotiated, and continually redefined. Featuring works by Diego Gabaldon, Gonzalo Hernandez, Sepideh Kalani, Shayla Marshall, Bex McCharen, Lucía Morales, Genesis Moreno, Ana Mosquera, Sheherazade Thénard, Pangea Kali Virga, Nadia Wolff, Ricardo E. Zulueta, and José Delgado Zuñiga.
records.curator: René Morales, Senior Curatorial Fellow, Bakehouse Art Complex
records.featuredArtists: Diego Gabaldon; Gonzalo Hernandez; Sepideh Kalani; Shayla Marshall; Bex McCharen; Lucía Morales; Genesis Moreno; Ana Mosquera; Sheherazade Thénard; Pangea Kali Virga; Nadia Wolff; Ricardo E. Zulueta; José Delgado Zuñiga

records.title: Open Studios / Sites of the Self / From Within
records.summary: Open Studios evening with exhibitions by 2026 Studio and Youth Artist Residents, July 8, 6–9 p.m.
records.description: Presented in conjunction with Oolite Arts' Open Studios, visitors are invited to engage with exhibitions developed directly with Oolite Arts' 2026 Studio and Youth Artist Residents. Guests will experience works-in-progress and gain insight into the evolving nature of contemporary artistic practice.

records.title: The Fabric of Remembering: Cyanotype & Quilting
records.address: 924 Lincoln Rd., First Floor, Miami Beach, FL 33139
records.summary: A three-week hands-on workshop with Bex McCharen exploring cyanotype printing on fabric, quilting, storytelling, and memory.
records.description: Join artist Bex McCharen for this three-week workshop exploring fabric as a vessel for memory, storytelling, and legacy. Participants will cyanotype print family photographs onto fabric, then piece, sew, and embellish the printed images into small quilted works. All skill levels welcome.
